stories.title: Truist Championship R2 — Sungjae Im Leads at 9-Under, McIlroy 4 Back

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Sungjae Im leads the Truist Championship at 9-under after 36 holes.

Rory McIlroy shot 67 to get within 4 shots.
Rickie Fowler's 63 (9 birdies!) puts him in the mix.

Round 3 today at Quail Hollow. Wide open. #TruistChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
McIlroy was 7 shots back after Round 1. Shot 67 in Round 2 to trim it to 4.

If ANYONE can charge up a leaderboard, it's Rory.

Seeking his 5th Quail Hollow title. This weekend just got interesting. #TruistChampionship

article.headline: Truist Championship R2 — Sungjae Im Surges to Lead, McIlroy Fires 67 to Get Back in It

research.facts:
Truist Championship R2 — Sungjae Im Leads at 9-Under
**Sources consulted:** WBTV/NBC affiliate Charlotte, Golf Channel/GolfChannel.com, Heavy.com, CBS Sports
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Sungjae Im leads at 9-under 133 after 36 holes [HIGH — WBTV NBC Charlotte]
- Rory McIlroy shot 4-under 67 in R2; now at 5-under, 4 shots back of Im [HIGH — WBTV NBC Charlotte]
- Rickie Fowler shot 8-under 63 (day's low round; 9 birdies) in R2, T8 [HIGH — Heavy.com]
- Matt McCarty led after R1 with 63 (-8) [HIGH — multiple sources from May 8 pipeline]
- Round 3 on Saturday (today); Final Round on Sunday May 10 [HIGH — PGA Tour schedule]
- Event at Quail Hollow Club, Charlotte, NC [HIGH]
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Fowler's T8 position after R2 [MEDIUM — Heavy.com summary]
**Story angle:** Im surged to the lead while McIlroy climbed back into contention after an inconsistent R1.

stories.title: LIV Golf Virginia — Herbert's Historic 36-Hole Dominance, 6-Shot Lead After R2

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Lucas Herbert at LIV Golf Virginia:
Round 1: 64 (-8)
Round 2: 63 (-9, bogey-free)
Total: -17. 6-shot lead.

His Strokes Gained Total is a LIV Golf 36-hole RECORD. Final round today. #LIVGolf

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
While Rahm and DeChambeau are nowhere near the lead, a 30-year-old Australian named Lucas Herbert is about to claim his first LIV Golf win.

-17 through 36 holes. 6-shot lead. Historic round-by-round consistency. #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Golf Virginia — Herbert's Historic 17-Under Lead Is One Round From History

research.facts:
LIV Golf Virginia — Herbert's Historic 36-Hole Lead
**Sources consulted:** LIV Golf official site R1 and R2 recaps, Washington Times (two articles), Fox Sports, infinitysport.asia
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Herbert R1: 8-under 64, 2-shot lead [HIGH — LIV.com R1 recap + Washington Times May 7]
- Herbert R2: 9-under 63, bogey-free [HIGH — Washington Times May 8 + LIV.com R2 recap]
- Herbert leads at -17 through 36 holes [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Herbert's lead: 6 shots [HIGH — Washington Times headline + LIV.com R2 recap]
- Second place: Sergio Garcia [HIGH — LIV.com R2 recap]
- Herbert's Strokes Gained Total: +7.82 per round = LIV Golf 36-hole record [HIGH — LIV.com R2 recap]
- Largest 36-hole lead this season; tied for second-largest in LIV history [HIGH — LIV.com R2 recap]
- Rahm and DeChambeau both at -3 after R1, struggling [HIGH — from previous research]
- Event at Trump National Golf Club, Potomac Falls/Washington DC area [HIGH]
- LIV Golf Virginia is a 3-round event; Final round R3 is today (Saturday May 9) [HIGH]
**Story angle:** Herbert's historic 36-hole performance puts him on the verge of his first LIV Golf win.

stories.title: PGA Championship Preview — Aronimink, May 14-17, Scheffler's Redemption Bid

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
One week until the PGA Championship.

Scottie Scheffler skipped the Truist Championship JUST to prepare for Aronimink.

He's had 3 straight runner-ups. He's hungry. Aronimink suits him perfectly. May 14-17. #PGAChampionship

article.headline: PGA Championship Preview — Scheffler's Bold Preparation, McIlroy's Momentum, and the Aronimink Test

research.facts:
PGA Championship Preview
**Sources consulted:** Pipeline research from May 7-8; PGA Tour official; USGA data
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club, Newtown Square, PA, May 14-17 [HIGH]
- 154-player field [HIGH — from May 8 pipeline]
- Scheffler skipped Truist Championship to prepare for Aronimink [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Scheffler world No. 1, ~+350 favorite [HIGH — from May 8 pipeline]
- Three consecutive runner-ups: Masters (to McIlroy), RBC Heritage (playoff to Fitzpatrick), Cadillac Championship [HIGH]
- McIlroy defending major champion (won Masters April 12) [HIGH]
- Donald Ross design; last PGA Championship at Aronimink: 1962, Gary Player won [HIGH — from May 8 pipeline]
- LIV stars confirmed: DeChambeau, Rahm, Dustin Johnson [HIGH — from May 8 pipeline]
- Two spots still available for Myrtle Beach + Truist winners [HIGH — from May 8 pipeline]
**Story angle:** With a week until tee-time, the major chess match is playing out — Scheffler prepping, McIlroy in-form at Truist, Rahm/DeChambeau using LIV Virginia as warm-up.

stories.title: McIlroy's Quail Hollow Record Quest — Five Under, Alive Heading Into Round 3

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy wants a 5th Quail Hollow title — no one has ever won 5 at this course.

He was 7 shots back. Shot 67. Now 4 shots back heading into Round 3.

The record is still very much alive. #TruistChampionship

article.headline: McIlroy's 67 Keeps Quail Hollow Record Bid Alive — Four Shots Back With Two Rounds to Play

McIlroy's Quail Hollow Record Quest
**Sources consulted:** Previous pipeline research; WBTV R2 update
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**

- R1 at Truist: 70 (1-under) [HIGH — from May 8 pipeline]
- R2 at Truist: 67 (4-under) [HIGH — WBTV NBC Charlotte]
- Current total: 5-under [HIGH — WBTV NBC Charlotte]
- 4 shots back of Im [HIGH — WBTV NBC Charlotte]
- Scheffler skipped event [HIGH — multiple sources]
**Story angle:** McIlroy's quiet comeback — from 1-under R1 to genuinely in contention at 5-under — sets up a weekend record chase.

stories.title: Nelly Korda HOF Countdown — Four Points Away, Kroger Queen City Is Next

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda needs 4 points to reach the LPGA Hall of Fame.

She has 18 career wins at 27 years old. The youngest American to get there since Nancy Lopez in 1980.

Next event: Kroger Queen City, May 14-17. History is watching. #LPGA

research.facts:
Nelly Korda HOF Countdown
**Sources consulted:** LPGA.com official HOF countdown articles, Yahoo Sports, GolfChannel.com, JustWomensSports
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Korda at 23/27 LPGA HOF points [HIGH — LPGA.com official article]
- 4 away from qualification [HIGH — LPGA.com headline]
- Next event: Kroger Queen City Championship, Cincinnati, May 14-17 [HIGH — search result]
- 18 career LPGA wins [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Youngest American to 18 wins since Nancy Lopez in 1980 [HIGH — LPGA.com + multiple sources]
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- LPGA HOF point values: win = +2 pts, major win = +3 pts [MEDIUM — calculated from known HOF progression]
**Story angle:** Four points to golf immortality — the Kroger Queen City is Korda's next shot at history.
